title: AIVS-Micro Attestation
description: The minimal 6-field AIVS-Micro attestation (~200 bytes). A single-URL scan proof designed for continuous monitoring, embedded widgets, API responses, DNS TXT records, and cold outreach attachments where a full session bundle is impractical. An Ed25519 signature over the canonical pipe-delimited payload provides tamper-evidence. See AIVS v1.0 Section 7.

url.description: The URL that was scanned. Used verbatim in the canonical signing payload.

dom_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the page DOM content at scan time, prefixed with 'sha256:'. The hash is computed over the raw DOM bytes. Used verbatim (including prefix) in the canonical signing payload.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of the scan with nanosecond precision. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS.nnnnnnnnnZ. Used verbatim in the canonical signing payload.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical payload '{url}|{dom_hash}|{timestamp}|{scanner_version_hash}|{scan_origin}', prefixed with 'ed25519:' followed by a Base64-encoded 64-byte signature. Value is the literal string 'unsigned' when no identity key is configured.

oneOf.description: No identity key configured. Hash-chain integrity is still present; identity binding is absent.
Base64-encoded Ed25519 signature (64 bytes = 88 base64 chars without padding, but standard base64 of 64 bytes = 88 chars). Prefixed with 'ed25519:'.

scanner_version_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the scanner implementation file (e.g., conduit_proof.py), prefixed with 'sha256:'. Binds this attestation to a specific, auditable version of the scanning code. Used verbatim (including prefix) in the canonical signing payload.

scan_origin.description: Where the scan originated. Implementations SHOULD use 'local' for on-device scans. Other values are implementation-defined. Used verbatim in the canonical signing payload.
